description:
An individual’s annual income results from various factors. Intuitively, it is influenced by the individual’s education level, age, gender, occupation, and etc. 

This is a widely cited KNN dataset. I encountered it during my course, and I wish to share it here because it is a good starter example for data pre-processing and machine learning practices. 

**Fields**
The dataset contains 16 columns
Target filed: Income
-- The income is divide into two classes: <=50K and >50K  
Number of attributes: 14
-- These are the demographics and other features to describe a person

We can explore the possibility in predicting income level based on the individual’s personal information.

**Acknowledgements**
This dataset named “adult” is found in the UCI machine learning repository
[http://www.cs.toronto.edu/~delve/data/adult/desc.html][1]

The detailed description on the dataset can be found in the original UCI documentation
[http://www.cs.toronto.edu/~delve/data/adult/adultDetail.html][2]

  [1]: http://www.cs.toronto.edu/~delve/data/adult/desc.html
  [2]: http://www.cs.toronto.edu/~delve/data/adult/adultDetail.html

results.reason: Directly encodes age, which is an explicit in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Employment class can weakly reflect socioeconomic status but does not directly encode it.
Sampling weight used for statistical purposes; does not represent any individual characteristic.
Education level can act as a weak proxy for socioeconomic status but does not directly encode it.
Numeric encoding of education level, which may weakly proxy socioeconomic status.
Directly encodes marital status, which is an explicit in-scope sensitive characteristic under family and civil status.
Occupation can weakly reflect socioeconomic status but does not directly encode a protected characteristic.
Encodes familial relationship roles, which fall under family and civil status.
Directly encodes racial origin, which is an explicit in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Directly encodes gender, which is an explicit in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Financial gain may weakly proxy socioeconomic status but does not directly encode it.
Financial loss may weakly proxy socioeconomic status but does not directly encode it.
Work hours reflect labor patterns but do not encode an in-scope sensitive characteristic.
Encodes national origin, which is an explicit in-scope sensitive characteristic under racial and ethnic origin.
Directly represents economic status, which is an explicit in-scope sensitive characteristic under socioeconomic status.
